title: Intense Italian Liver Saltimbocca
description: A bitter, snappy broiled liver dish with prosciutto and sage, served in scallop shells and finished with a poached egg, perfect for a sick day Italian meal.
instructions: Clean scallop shells and lightly grease them.
Season 200g chicken liver with salt and pepper.
Wrap each liver slice with a slice of prosciutto and a sage leaf, securing with a toothpick.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a broiler-safe skillet over medium-high until hot.
Sear the wrapped livers for 2 minutes per side until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the broiler and broil the livers for 3 minutes until cooked through.
While the livers broil, poach four eggs in simmering water for 3 minutes.
Remove livers from the skillet and place one in each scallop shell.
Drizzle 1/4 cup white wine, 2 tbsp butter, and a pinch of salt over the livers.
Top each liver with a poached egg and sprinkle with grated parmesan, then garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
